behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score
behaviors.justification: The real failure is a filtering logic bug: the availability check is skipped or inverted. This can be caught by a unit test that passes product objects with availability flags and verifies the filter excludes unavailable products. No I/O dependency is required—availability is an attribute on the product object itself.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Create test product objects with availability flags (mix of available=true and false). Run the filtering stage of the recommendation engine. Verify that products with available=false are excluded from results, even if they would rank highest by relevance.

behaviors.description: User preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the preference filter is missing, skipped, or applies the wrong logic (e.g., inverts the block list). Unit test with mocked user settings and test products catches this. The blocked categories are input parameters, not a complex data dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Create test user object with blocked_categories=['adult', 'weapons']. Create product objects from various categories including blocked and allowed. Run recommendation filter. Verify all products in blocked categories are excluded, and allowed products remain.

behaviors.description: Recommendation algorithm ranks products matching recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal
behaviors.justification: Real failure: algorithm ignores browsing history in ranking, or weights it lower than other signals. Unit test with mocked browsing history and products verifies ranking logic. The algorithm takes browsing history as input data, not a complex external dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Create test user with browsing history for product categories [Electronics, Home]. Create candidate products: some from Electronics/Home (browsed), some from Garden/Sports (not browsed). Run ranking algorithm. Verify products from browsed categories appear higher in the ranked list.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page of results with stable ordering; requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if underlying data unchanged
behaviors.justification: Real failure: offset/limit calculation is wrong, or ordering is non-deterministic (e.g., hash-based, timestamp-dependent). Unit test with fixed product list verifies pagination math and determinism. Deterministic output with fixed input is verifiable without I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Create fixed sorted list of 100 test products. Call paginate(products, page=2, page_size=10) twice. Verify both calls return products 11-20 in identical order. Test additional pages (3, 4, 5) to verify offset calculations correct.

behaviors.description: Algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category user hasn't browsed before (serendipity), avoiding filter bubble
behaviors.justification: Real failure: serendipity logic is missing (all results from browsed categories only). Unit test with mocked category data verifies the algorithm includes at least one product from unbrowsed categories. The algorithm takes category membership and browsing history as inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Create test user with browsing history only in categories [Tech, Books]. Create product catalog spanning 5 categories [Tech, Books, Sports, Art, Food]. Run recommendation algorithm. Verify results include at least one product from [Sports, Art, or Food].

behaviors.description: New user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity, not empty result set
behaviors.justification: Real failure: returns empty results, crashes on missing history, or serves wrong user's data. Unit test creates a new-user object with no history and verifies the fallback to popularity-based ranking works. Popularity ranking is computable from test product data.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Create new user object with empty purchase_history and empty browsing_history. Create test products with popularity scores. Run recommendation engine. Verify non-empty result set returned, ranked by popularity metrics (sales count, rating) rather than personalized signals.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, position in list, and timestamp for each recommendation user interacts with
behaviors.justification: Real failure: click events recorded incorrectly or not persisted to storage (losing training data). Pure unit mock of click storage would pass even if persistence is broken. Must test with real or realistic data store to ensure clicks actually persist for downstream training use.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: Simulate 5 recommendation interactions (each with product_id, position, timestamp). Call track_click() for each. Query the click storage (database or persistent store) and verify all 5 clicks present with accurate product IDs, positions (0-indexed), and timestamps (within 1 second of actual click time).

behaviors.description: Response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for catalog of 100,000 products
behaviors.justification: Real failure: algorithm or data pipeline doesn't scale; mocking hides performance issues (e.g., N² loops, unoptimized queries). Must test with realistic scale (100k products) in a real or representative database to measure actual performance. Unit tests at small scale miss performance regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: Load 100,000 products into test database with realistic attributes and indices. Run recommendation request for test user. Measure wall-clock time from request start to complete response. Execute 50 times and verify 95th percentile latency under 200ms. If exceeded, profile to identify bottleneck (filtering, sorting, DB query).

behaviors.description: When recommended product is purchased, algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests
behaviors.justification: Real failure: purchase recorded but deprioritization logic is skipped, or purchase state doesn't persist between requests. Mocking the purchase-state storage would pass even if state isn't actually saved. Must test with persistent storage to catch state-change failures.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: Request recommendations for test user, capture top-ranked product A. Record purchase of product A. Request recommendations again with same user state. Verify product A either absent or ranked significantly lower (e.g., not in top 10, deprioritized by score ≥0.3 points).

behaviors.description: Recommendation results are deterministic for same user state; requesting twice with no intervening activity returns identical ranked list
behaviors.justification: Real failure: algorithm uses randomization, hash-based ordering, or timestamp-dependent logic. Unit test with fixed input data (no intervening changes) verifies algorithm produces consistent output. Determinism is testable at function level with controlled input.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: Request recommendations for test user 10 times in rapid succession with no intervening user activity or data changes. Verify all 10 responses return identical product_ids in identical order. If any response differs, identify source of non-determinism (seed, shuffle, timestamp).
